title: Semantic editing + jumpCuts/cutSilences differentiation + Premiere API audit

stories.title: Premiere Pro API compliance audit
stories.description: Audit bridge-premiere.js and host/premiere.jsx against real CEP/ExtendScript Premiere Pro API. Document any findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Every CSInterface evalScript call in bridge-premiere.js maps to a defined function in host/premiere.jsx
Every function in host/premiere.jsx uses only real Premiere Pro ExtendScript API methods (app.project, QE, sequence.videoTracks, markers, etc.)
Marker API usage matches app.project.activeSequence.markers.createMarker / marker.type / marker.setTypeAsChapter semantics
Razor/trim operations use real track.insertClip / track.clips / qe.razor APIs
Audit report saved to .omc/research/premiere-api-audit.md with found issues and safe usage confirmation

stories.title: Differentiate jumpCuts and cutSilences (R11-R15)
stories.description: Extract shared silence detector, give each pipeline distinct concept: cutSilences = hygiene (≥1s), jumpCuts = rhythm (agressive, keep breathing).
acceptanceCriteria: Shared helper detectSilenceIntervals(entry, {thresholdDb, minDuration, padding, source}) exists in deterministic-pipelines.js
jumpCuts uses threshold gating (same logic as cutSilences) — no silences from below threshold
jumpCuts supports keepBreathing parameter (default 0.05s) — cuts leave breathing gap, not zero
jumpCuts supports minSegmentDuration parameter (default 0.3s) — adjacent intervals with tiny gap between merge
UI card subtitles differentiate: cutSilences=«гигиена, ≥1с», jumpCuts=«ритм, YouTube-стиль»
Unit tests pass: npm test shows all tests green, with new cases covering keepBreathing and minSegmentDuration

stories.title: Fix digression routing + add aggressiveness (R4)
stories.description: digression label must route to toRemove by default. Add aggressiveness parameter for granular control.
acceptanceCriteria: analyze_transcript_for_cuts accepts aggressiveness: 'gentle'|'normal'|'aggressive' (default 'normal')
gentle: only filler+artifact go to toRemove; intro/outro/outtake/repeat/digression stay in toKeep
normal: filler+artifact+intro+outro+outtake+repeat go to toRemove; digression stays in toKeep (current behavior preserved)
aggressive: all non-content labels (including digression) go to toRemove
Tool description in panel.js updated to document aggressiveness parameter
Unit test covers all 3 aggressiveness levels with mocked label set

stories.title: keepIntervals support in propose_transcript_cuts (R1)
stories.description: Let LLM specify what to KEEP, auto-invert to removeIntervals. Critical for «собери ролик про X» flow.
acceptanceCriteria: propose_transcript_cuts tool schema accepts keepIntervals as alternative to removeIntervals
If keepIntervals provided: executor computes removeIntervals = complement within [minSec, maxSec] of transcript
Inversion respects segment/paragraph boundaries — no cutting mid-word
If both keepIntervals and removeIntervals provided — error message to LLM
Tool description explicitly documents keepIntervals usage for assembly-style edits
Unit test: given keepIntervals [[10,20],[40,50]] on 60s transcript, removeIntervals = [[0,10],[20,40],[50,60]]

stories.title: Adaptive chapter min-interval + name validation (R7, R9)
stories.description: Chapter spacing adapts to duration; reject boilerplate names like «Часть N».
acceptanceCriteria: chapterize uses adaptive min-interval: <3min=10s, 3-10min=20s, >10min=45s (replaces hardcoded 15s)
Chapter names matching /^(Часть|Part)\s*\d+$|^Продолжение$|^Следующ.+ часть$/i are rejected and replaced with first 4 words of first paragraph in chapter
Fallback time-based chapters also get meaningful names (first words of nearest segment), not «Часть N»
Unit test verifies adaptive interval for 3 duration tiers
Unit test verifies name validation rejects «Часть 2», replaces with real text

stories.title: Update prompts.js + full regression test
stories.description: Wire new capabilities into prompts.js so LLM knows to use them. Full test regression.
acceptanceCriteria: prompts.js TIER1_TRANSCRIPT documents keepIntervals (R1) — «для сборки используй keepIntervals»
prompts.js TIER1_TRANSCRIPT documents aggressiveness parameter (R4) — «по умолчанию normal»
prompts.js version marker or comment updated to reflect new capabilities
npm test — all tests pass (new + existing)
No lsp diagnostics errors on modified files (client/shared/*.js, client/unified/panel.js)
